summary: Bulk merge of #2491 and #2494-#2499, mainly decomp/matching work across stage files and lb library modules. The visible build change moved src/melee/gr/grheal.c from NonMatching to Matching. decomp.dev reported 22 new matches, including grHeal_8021F180, lbMemory_8001564C, grHeal_8021F70C, several lbsnap routines, grzebesroute data/rodata/sdata/sdata2, and +5172 matched-code bytes; grZebesRoute_8020B548 was implemented to 99.97% but still listed as an unmatched improvement.

key_files.why: Marks melee/gr/grheal.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Adds inferred data-block layouts, explicit rodata/sdata/sbss symbols, grHeal_8021F180 implementation, and grHeal_8021F70C/EFEC matching adjustments.
Adds stage data/format strings/constants and implements grZebesRoute_8020B548 light setup nearly matching.
Reworks ARQ node state handling with enum values, stack padding, and dont_inline getter.
Defines lbArqState and concrete lbArqGlobal node array layout.
Defines concrete allocator structures, named assert strings, implements lbMemory_80015320 and lbMemory_8001564C, and improves AR/DevCom alarm-copy logic.
Clarifies Handle.x8_hi as either arena high bound or child allocation size.
Introduces heap descriptor/offset-view helpers and rewrites heap reset/destroy/create flow for better matching.
Adds inline text/tiled-offset/save-data helpers and improves several snapshot-card routines.
Uses lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries directly instead of a local alias to improve lb_8001B14C.

what_changed: PR body is a checklist-style bulk merge for #2491, #2494, #2495, #2496, #2497, #2498, and #2499.
configure.py changed grheal.c from Object(NonMatching, "melee/gr/grheal.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/gr/grheal.c").

grheal gained inferred composite data types grHeal_UnkVec4, grHeal_DataBlock, and grHeal_StageData with STATIC_ASSERT sizes, replacing raw/static data with explicit globals for stage callbacks, StageData-plus-format strings, assert filename text, sdata "gobj", and grHeal_804D6AF0[2].

grHeal_8021F180 was implemented: it animates the ground object, periodically spawns a coin using it_802F2094 after Ground_801C2D24/Ground_801C5840, builds next-player and player JObj arrays, reparents/animates next-player/player models, installs mpJointSetCb1, and resets gp->gv.flatzone2.xC4.

grHeal_8021F70C was rewritten into explicit gotos and table-walking over grHeal_803E83B8, including the character_id 0x13 -> 0x12 special case and OSReport through the inferred DataBlock report string.
grzebesroute gained grZebesRoute_LightData, explicit stage data/string/sdata/sdata2 constants, and changed OSReport/assert users to named data instead of __FILE__/literal strings.

grZebesRoute_8020B548 was implemented to configure spot/point HSD_LObj lights from scaled positions, colors, spot attenuation, and distance attenuation constants; decomp.dev still listed it as a 99.97% unmatched improvement rather than a full match.

lbarq added lbArqState enum values LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE/PENDING/DONE, typed lbArqNode.state, concrete lbArqGlobal.nodes[10], enum-indexed list handling, PAD_STACK(16), and #pragma dont_inline around lbArq_80014ABC.

lbmemory replaced anonymous padding with concrete MemEntry/LBMgr/Allocator layouts, exported lbMemory_804318B0 via g_alloc macro, added named rodata/sdata assert strings, implemented lbMemory_80015320 relocation/copy callback logic, and implemented lbMemory_8001564C AR allocator initialization under #pragma dont_inline.
lbheap introduced lbHeap_HeapDesc, lbHeap_HeapOffsetView, and inline helpers for heap reset/destroy/create, then rewrote lbHeap_80015900 and lbHeap_80015F3C around those layouts.

lbsnap added small static inline helpers for zeroing 0x21-byte text buffers, tiled RGB/Y offsets, and save-data offset calculation; several routines now use a local ret variable and direct helper calls for better codegen.
lbcardnew removed a local snapshot_entries pointer and writes through lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries directly.
smart_moves: Using composite structs with STATIC_ASSERTs for grHeal data captured the real contiguous layout of arrays, callbacks, StageData, and format strings without pretending each symbol is independent.
Replacing magic ARQ list indices 0/1/2 with lbArqState kept the original numeric layout while making list/state intent searchable and safer.
Named rodata/sdata strings for assert and OSReport calls allowed source to express original data placement instead of relying on __FILE__ or string literal pooling.
Small static inline helpers in lbheap/lbsnap improved readability while still allowing expansion needed for matching-sensitive codegen.
The lbmemory allocator was modeled as a real 0x6F0 struct with MemEntry, Handle array, and LBMgr fields, making offset-dependent code like x6A0_mgr and free-list initialization explicit.
The PR kept exact codegen levers visible where needed: #pragma dont_inline, PAD_STACK, UNUSED locals, explicit gotos, casts, and pointer arithmetic.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, StageData often sits next to report/assert strings; matching data may require wrapper structs such as grHeal_StageData or ad hoc stage-data blobs with trailing fmt fields.

When a table is emitted as one data block, it can be legitimate to access later fields through casts or byte offsets, as in grHeal_803E83B8 + 0x9C and ((grHeal_DataBlock*) base)->report_fmt_not_found_next_player.
Do not assume cleaner C control flow will match: grHeal_8021F70C needed explicit labels/gotos and a separate loop_check/loop_compare shape.
For library allocators, concrete global layout and freelist element counts matter; replacing padding with arrays like MemEntry x8_mem[0x83] and Handle x638_heap[6] unlocks both code and data matches.
Use named small-data strings and constants when literals affect section placement or instruction selection; this PR did that in grheal, grzebesroute, and lbmemory.
Header changes should expose inferred types/globals only where cross-file use requires them, as with grHeal_UnkData/grHeal_804D6AF0 and lbArqState/lbArqGlobal.

decomp.dev can distinguish full matches from near-matches: grZebesRoute_8020B548 was a large +779-byte improvement but still not fully matched, so keep the nonmatching marker until the report reaches 100%.
naming_conventions: Stage globals preserve file prefixes and address-style suffixes, e.g. grHeal_803E83B8, grHeal_803E84C4, grZe_Route_803E5E0C.
Unknown/inferred structs use scoped descriptive names with Unk when semantics are incomplete: grHeal_UnkVec4, grHeal_UnkData, grZebesRoute_LightData.
Composite layout helpers are named for the owning module and purpose: grHeal_DataBlock, grHeal_StageData, lbHeap_HeapOffsetView.
ARQ state enum values use all-caps module prefix and semantic state names: LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE, LB_ARQ_STATE_PENDING, LB_ARQ_STATE_DONE.
Raw labels for known data use lbl_address style when no semantic name is available, e.g. lbl_803BA2C0 for "lbmemory.c".
Small-data declarations are explicit with SDATA char names such as grHeal_804D49E0, grZe_Route_804D4840, and lbMemory_804D3788.
Function declarations in headers retain address comments like /* 4D6AF0 */ extern grHeal_UnkData* grHeal_804D6AF0[2].
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop was used around lbArq_80014ABC and lbMemory_8001564C.
PAD_STACK was added or adjusted in lbArq_80014BD0, lbArq_80014D2C, and lbsnap routines to shape stack frames.
Explicit gotos and labels in grHeal_8021F70C preserved the target loop structure better than a while loop.
Pointer arithmetic and casted views modeled compiler addressing: (StageCallbacks*) ((char*) grHeal_803E83B8 + 0x9C), ((s16*) grHeal_803E83B8)[i], and lbHeap_GetHeapOffsetView(offset).
UNUSED locals such as reserved_next_jobj/reserved_player_jobj and a pad array in grZebesRoute_8020B548 were kept to influence stack/register layout.
Named const float/GXColor symbols in grzebesroute moved immediates into the expected rodata/sdata2 locations.
Direct global field access, such as lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries[j], can match better than a convenient local alias.
review_feedback: No human reviews or review comments were present in the provided slice.

The decomp.dev bot report validated the merge with +0.13% matched code (+5172 bytes), +0.06% linked code (+2312 bytes), +0.08% matched data (+984 bytes), and +0.04% linked data (+464 bytes) for GALE01.

Bot report listed 22 new matches, including grHeal_8021F180, lbMemory_8001564C, grHeal_8021F70C, lbSnap_8001DF6C, lbSnap_8001D7B0, lbSnap_8001D5FC, lbSnap_8001E058, and grheal/grzebesroute data sections.

Bot report listed 6 unmatched improvements, notably grZebesRoute_8020B548 from 0.00% to 99.97%, lb_8001B14C to 98.77%, lbMemory_80015320 to 96.11%, lbHeap_80015900 to 96.48%, lbSnap_8001DA5C to 55.88%, and lbArq_80014BD0 to 96.55%.
follow_up_queries: Inspect constituent PRs #2491 and #2494-#2499 if individual author rationale or review discussion is needed; this bulk PR slice only shows the combined merge.
Search future PRs for grZebesRoute_8020B548 to find the final 99.97% -> 100% fix.
Search grHeal_DataBlock and grHeal_StageData for reusable patterns when StageData is adjacent to OSReport/assert strings.
Search lbHeap_HeapOffsetView and lbHeap_GetHeapOffsetView for heap-offset view tactics in other allocator code.
Search lbMemory_80015320 for later cleanup or full match status, since this PR only improved it to 96.11%.
Search lbSnap_GetTiledRGBOffset and lbSnap_GetTiledYOff for reusable tiled-texture address math in snapshot/icon code.
